entry.title: Re: Attachments2 using HttpClient and C#

entry.title: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a requirement where i have to continue batch job even after error and the job should not cancel and the message should appear as error not like information or warning in the job log.

entry.title: Automatic Subaccount Migration to another Region
entry.content:
Hi All!

Besides the SAP Note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003041304 with instructions on how to migrate a subaccount's contents from one region to another, Is there any tool to automate at least part of this migration process?

entry.title: Re: Cheat sheet for URI patterns for calling OData actions and functions
entry.content:
I have now been able to put together a working OData V4 URL for the static function. I have understood, that in V4 my static function was bound to a collection.

So the syntax is <pathToService>/<EntitySet>/<Schema Namespace>.<functionName>

My above example works with this URL:

entry.title: Re: Where can I find the change number used in MM12 trans...
entry.content: Thanks, Ankurch, but again, the change is scheduled (MM12) but has not yet happened.  I can see the change in MM14, but I cannot see the change number used to make the change.  After the change has been executed, then it will show up under the material in Display Changes.  At this point, there does not appear to be a good way to see what the change number is that was used for the MM12 transaction.

entry.title: Process Automation: Unattended agent is disconnected automatically
entry.content:
Hi all,

I created an automated process in SAP Build Process Automation. The process is started with a scheduled timer. The process works exactly fine when my computer is active and I can see that the agent is active.

However, when I do not have my computer "active", the agent is automatically put to disconnected. When I go to Monitor > Automated Jobs, I can see that the job is set to "Ready" with the comment "Your job cannot be distributed because no unattended agent is available". Also, if I put the agent to unattended and close my computer, I get the same error because the agent is set to "Disconnected".

Is it possible to let an agent run the process even though your computer is off?

Thanks,

Stefanie

entry.title: Re: in Odata metadata not loaded it is not activating
entry.content: more detail.

entry.title: Re: How many user use Single SAP ID at same time SAP Business One
entry.content: SAP ID means , User which has assigned SAP B1 license to use SAP B1

entry.title: Goods receipt posting price based on supplier confirmed price EDI1 condition
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a business case, where the supplier confirms for PO placed and after confirmation IDoc ORDRSP processing, EDI1 condition updates into PO but during GR, the EDI1 condition is not picking instead PB00 condition is picked for WRX account.

Can you please help how to route the posting to pick the EDI1 condition price as priority otherwise PB00 as next condition.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Missing Adaptation after Deployment to Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
you don't have to create semantic object, you just have to remove the standard target mapping from your role.

make sure  semantic objct+action point only to your z target mapping

Is modifying custom field logic in purchase item able to retrieve sales order item's information?

Customer requires to copy the sales item's long text from SO to PR custom shipment remark field when it is created from MRP. Is this possible?

entry.title: Re: occurs obsolete abap
entry.content: DATA: gt_test Type table of ZTEST.

entry.title: Fields Kenn1 and kenn2 in IT2001 are cleared, when changing Sick-Leave-End-Day with PA61
entry.content:
Ich habe folgendes Problem: Wenn man bei einer Krankheit etwas mit PA61 ändert, z.B. das Enddatum anpasst, werden die Felder "kenn1" und "kenn2" geleert.

Es könnte sein, dass die SAP-Hinweise 734338 und 1229347 damit zusammenhängen. In diesen Hinweisen wird folgender Code hinzugefügt:

  • CLEAR:
    p2001-kenn1,
    p2001-kenn2.
  • und PERFORM fill_switch TABLES tbfast.

Hat jemand schon ähnliche Erfahrungen gemacht?

Vielen Dank im Voraus!

ENGLISH:

Here is my problem: When editing a sickness record using PA61, for example by changing the end date, the fields "kenn1" and "kenn2" are being cleared.

It might be related to the solutions provided in SAP Notes 734338 and 1229347, which added the following code:

  • CLEAR:
    p2001-kenn1,
    p2001-kenn2.
  • und PERFORM fill_switch TABLES tbfast.

Has anyone else encountered this issue?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: How to get started with LLMs?
entry.content:
Join AI experts Daniel Dahlmeier (Chief Data Scientist, SAP) and Ted Way (VP and Chief Product Officer, SAP) as they explore the exciting world of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Generative AI. In this interview, they answer key questions on getting started with LLMs, evaluating use cases, and choosing the right models. Learn about prompt engineering, RAG, and fine-tuning, and discover the advantages of SAP's Generative AI Hub. They’ll also discuss future trends, how generative AI could transform work, and where the hype may be overblown. Perfect for AI enthusiasts and professionals, this video offers valuable insights and practical advice. Don't miss it! - And yes, this abstract was written with the help of an LLM 🙂

Speaker: Daniel Dahlmeier and Ted Way, SAP

entry.title: linking inspection requirements to a network activity (ie cords, mords, production orders, pboms)
entry.content:
because i don't know if this functionality exists its difficult to point to a specific area or areas to focus on in sap. as noted below, we are outsourcing certain items out of the company. when they come back in from the supplier, we need our internal quality inspectors to be turned on to inspect the outsourcing vendor's work. what steps are needed to make this happen if again the functionality exists? is it all done in the different order types or is it done in the material views of the part numbers or both?

Sincerely,

Adam Crane

Background/History:

We are having a requirement to see if there's a possibility to create inspection lot at 'Network - Activity' level, or just use the activity level to perform inspection, for activities that were normally "In-house" processing but are now being outsourced. We need sap to kick off a inspection requirement when the parts come back in so that the user can perform, if required, and record the Inspection information against a Network - Activity. Ex - Network Activity: 1) Plate cuttingu2014 Inspect cut edges 2)Welding - Inspect Weld joint 3)Rolled tube - Check Inner diameter

Katherine Kenneally

Hi Adam, which SAP topic are you referring to - is it digital manufacturing? You would be better off asking the experts in the Q&A section of our community. Please select your topic under our Products and Technology area. This would be the best approach since I am an SAP Community Advocate and not...

adam_crane

  1. its the networks/orders associated with manufacturing parts in-house. we want to outsource some of those items but when they come back to us to be placed back into the value stream we need sap through the activities in the network/orders to cause someone to perform certain types of inspections before it goes on to the next stage of the build. can you recommend an area to send my concern too? thank you for replying to my message. i have been trying all areas within the sap community for a very long time and you are the 1st person to get back to me; been apart of the sap community for at least 20 years

Community Advocate Katherine Kenneally

4 hours ago

I'm sorry to hear that nobody got back to you - let me try to find some leads for you, to support. Here are some ideas below:

 

Where it says "Ask a Question" on the Enterprise Resource Planning Q&A - you could try asking your question there.

There is an Enterprise Architecture group which has a Knowledge Base - you could try joining that group and connecting with members there, like:

Gabor_Kovacs: https://community.sap.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/16435 or

PaulKurchina: https://community.sap.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/5786

Perhaps they can point you in the right direction?

Let me know how you get on,

Thanks,

Katherine

entry.title: Re: CAP Project Deployment Error : "restrict_all_services": false in package.json file
entry.content:
Hi @sPatil, the following code tells 4 things to the CAP application:

  •   "[production]":  the following settings only apply when deploying to "production", which truly means when running on BTP rather on your localhost
  • "auth": settings regarding authorization
  • "kind": "xsuaa": the type of authorization. It will use the xsuaa service from BTP
  • "restrict_all_services": false : means that authorization will not be applied to your services. Users don't need to be logged on neither need roles. This setting is useful for debugging, especially when something works locally but not when running on BTP.

entry.title: Extend CDS View field not visible in Field Control BADI for Manage PO App
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I have been extended the C_PurchaseOrderItemTP with Custom Field and we would like to control based on the certain condition so I have implemented the BADI for Controlling. however I dont see the Custom field. We have other scenario where extended via Custom Field to the Business Context, it is showing for control.

Do we need to do anything on the BOPF or CDS Level to control this.    

hanks and Regards,

Bharani

entry.title: Re: Attachment size control

entry.title: Re: How to query a customer's credit exposure data throug...
entry.content: Please check SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3517767

entry.title: Re: How to allow an empty string with check condition in xml transformation?
entry.content:
What is important in your case is that you are using a deserialization, so in that case <tt:cond s-check="..."> behaves like <tt:cond> which means that the transformation accepts an XML where the tag <branchCode> is absent, otherwise the value is transferred to the ABAP component BRANCHCODE (whatever it's empty or one space or any other value). I guess that you have an issue because of the next tags.

I have a test program in which I tried the code below, and it works as expected:

 

METHOD test1.
  DATA(xml) ='<Invoice>                    '
          && '  <branchCode> </branchCode>'
          && '</Invoice>                   '.
  DATA(transfo_result) = VALUE ty_transfo_result( ).
  CALL TRANSFORMATION (transfo_name)
      SOURCE XML xml
      RESULT root = transfo_result.
  cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = transfo_result
                                      exp = VALUE ty_transfo_result( branchcode = '' ) ).
ENDMETHOD.
METHOD test2.
  DATA(xml) ='<Invoice>                    '
          && '  <branchCode></branchCode>'
          && '</Invoice>                   '.
  DATA(transfo_result) = VALUE ty_transfo_result( ).
  CALL TRANSFORMATION (transfo_name)
      SOURCE XML xml
      RESULT root = transfo_result.
  cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = transfo_result
                                      exp = VALUE ty_transfo_result( branchcode = '' ) ).
ENDMETHOD.
METHOD test3.
  DATA(xml) ='<Invoice>                    '
          && '  <branchCode>A</branchCode>'
          && '</Invoice>                   '.
  DATA(transfo_result) = VALUE ty_transfo_result( ).
  CALL TRANSFORMATION (transfo_name)
      SOURCE XML xml
      RESULT root = transfo_result.
  cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = transfo_result
                                      exp = VALUE ty_transfo_result( branchcode = 'A' ) ).
ENDMETHOD.
METHOD setup.
  transfo_name = create_transfo( concat_lines_of( sep = |\r\n| table = VALUE string_table(
( `<?sap.transform simple?>                                     ` )
( `<tt:transform                                                ` )
( `xmlns:tt="http://www.sap.com/transformation-templates"       ` )
( `xmlns:ddic="http://www.sap.com/abapxml/types/dictionary"     ` )
( `xmlns:def="http://www.sap.com/abapxml/types/defined">        ` )
( `  <tt:root name="ROOT"/>                                     ` )
( `                                                             ` )
( `  <tt:template>                                              ` )
( `    <Invoice>                                                ` )
( `      <tt:cond s-check="not-initial(ref('ROOT.BRANCHCODE'))">` )
( `        <branchCode tt:value-ref="ROOT.BRANCHCODE"/>         ` )
( `      </tt:cond>                                             ` )
( `    </Invoice>                                               ` )
( `  </tt:template>                                             ` )
( `                                                             ` )
( `</tt:transform>                                              ` ) ) ) ).
ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Receive SAP IBP Alerts in Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently working with SAP IBP and would like to know if it's possible to configure the system so that alerts generated in IBP can be received directly in Microsoft Teams. Is there any integration between SAP IBP and Teams, or would this require a third-party tool or customization?

If anyone has experience with this or can point me in the right direction, I would greatly appreciate it!

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Issue with Data Types: Batch Load Data into a Custom Business Object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

I referred below Blog to upload data into Custom Business Object using EXCEL.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/batch-load-data-into-a-custom-business-object-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13359205

Everything was working great except if custom business objects has any field with data types Quantity/Amount is not working while uploading data through excel.

@MartyMcCormick

API NW ABAP Gateway (OData)

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: How to set up SAP Cloud ALM Alerts for inaccessible URLs
entry.content:
I would like to set up SAP Cloud ALM to create alerts and send e-mails when a specific URL (such as the S/4 Fiori URL) becomes inaccessible.
I have checked the SAP documentation but couldn't find the information.
If there is a way to configure this, please share the method.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to set up SAP Cloud ALM Alerts for inaccessible URLs
entry.content:
No SAP Cloud ALM for Operations use case has functionality to perform a periodic availability ping for a generic URL, or generate alerts/e-mails for such a ping. 

Real User Monitoring and Synthetic User Monitoring offer some functionality related to performance and availability of applications on the web.
However, the application must meet the relevant Real User Monitoring requirements or Synthetic User Monitoring requirements.

Real User Monitoring:

  • Overview
  • Supported Content
  • Setup & Configuration

Synthetic User Monitoring:

  • Content
  • Setup & Configuration

Please note, Synthetic User Monitoring offers no alert or e-mail functionality. A user must access the app periodically to examine the results of script executions for insights.
Real User Monitoring only offers alerts/e-mails for general HTTP status errors and cannot be configured to create alerts/e-mails for specific URLs. But it does include availability statuses like:

  • 408 Request Timeout
  • 500 Internal Server Error
  • 503 Service Unavailable
  • 504 Gateway Timeout

entry.title: Re: The print button I created in the toolbar is not work...
entry.content: Yes, screen 0200 is what I called "normal Dynpro" and "classic Dynpro", opposed to CL_GUI_CONTAINER=>SCREEN0 which is not part of official programming (it's a trick, I don't know any SAP standard program using this).

entry.title: Creating a Single Warehouse Order for Multiple Customer Deliveries Using WOCR Without Wave Method.
entry.content:
Hello experts and Community Members,

Problem Description: We are dealing with an outbound scenario in SAP EWM where wave picking is not utilized. We have 10 customers, each with a single delivery for the same product.

Conditions to be Met:

  1. Outbound Scenario: No wave picking involved.
  2. Customers and Deliveries: 10 customers, each with 1 delivery for the same product.
  3. Warehouse Tasks: Need to create warehouse tasks for all 10 deliveries simultaneously.
  4. Warehouse Order: The goal is to have 1 warehouse order containing these 10 warehouse tasks.
  5. Method: Use WOCR (Warehouse Order Creation Rules) to achieve this, without employing the wave method.

Please suggest how to accomplish this in SAP EWM.

entry.title: Re: How to display ALV GRID with only structure
entry.content: Easy if you insert the structure into an internal table (display an internal table with one line).

entry.title: Responsive Smart Table AutoColumnWith with full screen size
entry.content:
Hi, we're currently creating Responsive UI5 applications.

I really like the concept of AutoColumnWidth adjusting the columns according to the content sizes. Also there is a nice table propery "customizeConfig" seting AutoColumnWith for all Columns

However, when I set enableAutoColumnwith="true", the table does not fill the sceen anymore.


This is a requirement for us and I wonder if it's possible to have a column adjust to the remaining screen size automatically

Any input is much appreciated!

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error

entry.title: Re: Problems with the Message Summary Alerts window
entry.content:
Hi serge34 

Please check if issue happen, after deleting the alerts for effected user.

Also check if you have huge number of alerts for effected user in OALR table.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: How to Monitor S/4 HANA DB unresponsiveness and S/4HANA Message Process Errors with SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content:
I would like to monitor S/4HANADB unresponsiveness and message service process errors using SAP Cloud ALM. However, it appears there are no monitoring thresholds for DB unresponsiveness and message service.
Is there a way to monitor and check the above settings?
If my approach is incorrect, please advise.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Monitor S/4 HANA DB unresponsiveness and S/4HA...
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring Content page outlines the metrics supported for various SAP products.
If you type "hana database" into the search bar on the page, it will list the Health Monitoring metrics that can be configured for that Product type. 
From my searches, I do not see any metrics related to database unresponsiveness or database message service process errors.

If you require specific metrics that are not yet supported for your Product, or a Product that you use is not yet supported, please check the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations campaign in the SAP Customer Influence portal.
Please check for pre-existing entries that match you requirement, and vote on them to show your support.
If no existing entry is found that matches your requirements, create a new one and outline the business case for the functionality.

entry.title: Re: What Are You Looking Forward to Learning or Doing at Devtoberfest This Year?

Looking forward to explore new topics about:

  • ABAP
  • AI
  • UI5

  • Fiori IOS SDK
  • Integration
  • SAP Build
  • And more about other technical areas.

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content: It seems there is no other way to handle error msg without stopping the job?

entry.title: SAP Development Reimagined: New Agile process with AI Tools, Clean Core, and Extensibility in SAP
entry.content:
Hey SAP enthusiasts!

If you've ever found yourself questioning the impact of AI on SAP roles, especially in the realm of ABAP, it's time to rethink that stance. We’re standing on the brink of something exciting! SAP is gearing up to revolutionize ABAP development with Joule—an innovation that showcases SAP's dedication to weaving AI into the very fabric of its ecosystem. it is already available for CAP - Cloud application Programming with Build Code. SAP will launch something for ABAP sooner or later. 

So, what does this mean for us in the SAP development landscape? Let’s dive into how AI is transforming our work lives and making the development process more efficient, innovative, and, dare I say, a bit more fun!

Why AI Matters in SAP Development and Clean Core

With the advent of SAP Cloud, extensibility models and the 'Clean Core' philosophy, there is a shift in how we approach SAP development. It’s no longer just about writing code; it’s about harnessing intelligent tools that can help us create better applications while keeping our core systems adaptable and resilient.

Imagine this: AI chatbots are not just quirky features anymore; they’re now our trusty companions that provide instant coding support. Sounds intriguing, right? Let’s explore some real-world scenarios that highlight this transformative journey.

 

Scenario 1: The Fast Track for Functional Specifications ( FS To Code )

Picture this: you upload all your Functional Specifications (FS) into a custom Fiori tool designed to streamline our development process. As the AI quickly grasps each FS, it sends an email to the developer assigned to the task. Imagine the thrill of receiving a Fiori Inbox notification that says, “New Development Task!” But here’s the kicker—you find that AI has already prewritten 20-40% of the objects and code you need. Talk about a head start!

Scenario 2: CoPilot for Code ( Code Assistant )

Now, let’s dive deeper. When you're tasked with writing an ABAP class, AI steps in to make your life easier.

  • Functional Requirement Clarification: No more endless back-and-forth with consultants! AI can explain and clarify functional requirements, saving you time and headaches.
  • AI-Based Code Completion: Ever wish you had a coding buddy who could predict your next move? AI tools do just that, filling in code as you type and minimizing those pesky errors.
  • Intelligent Commenting: You write the code, and AI does the rest—adding comments that enhance readability and maintainability. It’s like having a personal assistant who knows exactly what you need!

Scenario 3: Optimizing Your Code ( Assistance for Optimization and Conversion )

We all know that maintaining clean code is crucial. AI can help with that too!

  • Code Modularization: AI can suggest ways to break down complex code into manageable, reusable modules.
  • Redundancy Removal: Let’s get rid of the clutter! AI identifies and helps remove duplicate or unnecessary code, keeping your codebase streamlined.
  • HANA and ABAP Golden Rules: Logic and Context aware AI assistant can enforce application of golden rules of ABAP and SQL.
  • ECC to S/4HANA Code Conversion: If you’re dealing with legacy ECC code, AI can assist in converting it to meet S/4HANA standards, ensuring you’re ready for the cloud.

Scenario 4: Cloud Compliance Made Easy ( Dev Assistant for Clean Core )

With AI, compliance is a breeze. It integrates with Cloudification libraries or ABAP-Cloud-Readiness ATC Checks to ensure your code adheres to cloud standards. Plus, it can:

  • RAP-Based Fiori App Generation: AI assistant can transform your ABAP reports into RAP applications, generating Fiori Elements UI with minimal developer input. How cool is that?
  • Cloud Compliant Code: ATC Checks can find cloud readiness errors, but cannot solve it. AI tools can solve errors and make it cloud ready with single click. How? It knows what code is written for, and has analysis of ATC Checks or Cloudification library, it can replace deprecated APIs and other constructs with their successors. If no Successor is available, it can suggest work around of creating a wrapper with 3-tier extensibility model.

Scenario 5: AI to create Technical Specs from FS and Code ( Code To TS )

No Developer likes writing TS. Period.

  • Test Script Writing and Execution: Writing test scripts based on your code logic? AI has got your back, executing them to validate functionality.
  • TS Technical Specification and TUT Preparation: Say goodbye to writing TS and TUT Documents manually !  AI understands the overall context with Functional specs and code. It can create templates for us, moreover, it can write TS on behalf of us. From the test scripts, it can put placeholders for developer to paste screenshot once TUT is done.

Scenario 5: Seamless CI/CD Pipeline / Transport Management Integration and Automation

Integrating AI into your CI/CD pipelines / Transport Management can automate testing and deployment processes. Imagine spending less time on tedious tasks and more time on what you love—creating amazing SAP applications!

 

Technical Foundations for such AI Integration

Before SAP officially launches something and make our life easier working with SAP and ABAP, let me create  

To make all this magic happen, we need a solid foundation. Here are some key elements:

IDE 

  • Custom Solution with Fiori and CAP : Screens to upload FSs in UI5, NodeJS to assign it to one of the available developers, Integration with SolMan for project handling.Integration with ABAP system via OData exposure can provide all repositories of SAP. SAPUI5 already has CodeEditor UI Control that supports many languages including ABAP. It can be extended to add Custom Rules and add UI for a GenAI Assistant.

GenAI

  • Foundation LLM Model on SAP AICore and GenAI Hub: This is the backbone for our AI functionalities.
  • Grounding Techniques: Resources like help.sap.com, developers.sap.com, and the SAP Learning Hub provide essential knowledge and insights. 
  • Webhooks/Tools: By utilizing open-source UI5, CAP, ABAP Lints, and Cloudification libraries, we can ensure our code is cloud-ready.

Stay tuned for another blog on a tool developed with above design and for more Technical details. 

 

Integrating it with existing and widely adopted IDE would be more suited for enterprises.

  • Eclipse: An Add-on on top of ADT Tools with GenAI Assistant.
  • BAS: Tools such as Joule with Build Code already work well with CAP model on BTP, SAP can extend it to support ABAP Language and integrate with ABAP on Cloud.
  • VSCode: A new add on for ABAP with GenAI Assistant.

 

Conclusion:

The integration of AI into SAP development is not just a trend; it's a game changer that will empower us to work smarter, enhance our productivity, and ultimately deliver better solutions to our businesses.

Share your thoughts, experiences, or questions in the comments below. I’d love to hear how you envision AI changing your work life!

entry.title: Re: Default values via annotation for V4 action input parameters
entry.content:
Hi Pavan,

 

Is is possible to pass determined value as default value in Odata V4. 

Like value is coming from another CDS view etc.

I do not have possibility to use default function option which is only available in public cloud right now.

entry.title: Re: Backoffice: Change the order of locales on localized...
entry.content:
you need to extend com.hybris.cockpitng.editor.localized.LocalizedEditor and then in your custom editor you have to override getReadableLocales and getWriteableLocales methods

These method, in standard code, use HashSet which does not guarantee order.  When you override them you have to, for the retained locales, sort them and then collect them into a Set implementation that retains order e.g LinkedHashSet

 

@Override
	protected Set<Locale> getReadableLocales(final EditorContext editorContext)
	{
		return getLocales(editorContext.getReadableLocales());
	}

	@Override
	protected Set<Locale> getWriteableLocales(final EditorContext editorContext)
	{
		return getLocales(editorContext.getWritableLocales());
	}

	private Set<Locale> getLocales(final Set<Locale> contextValue)
	{
		final Set<Locale> locales = Optional.ofNullable(contextValue)
			.map(Sets::newHashSet)
			.orElseGet(Sets::newHashSet);
		final List<Locale> enabledDataLocales = getCockpitLocaleService().getEnabledDataLocales(getCockpitUserService().getCurrentUser());
		locales.retainAll(enabledDataLocales);
		return locales.stream()
			// Sort & Use LinkedHashSet to retain order
			// the call to this then ensures the session locale is rendered first
			// followed by all other locales, based on the order of the set
			.sorted(Comparator.comparing(Locale::toString))
			.collect(Collectors.toCollection(LinkedHashSet::new));
	}

 

 

After this you then need to create the definition.xml for your custom editor (basically copy the standard LocalizedEditor definition - below - and tweak it with your custom package and class name)

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!--
 Copyright (c) 2020 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company. All rights reserved
-->
<editor-definition id="com.hybris.cockpitng.editor.localized" << change the id
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="http://www.hybris.com/schema/cockpitng/editor-definition.xsd">

	<name>Localized Editor</name>
	<description>Localized Editor</description>
	<author>hybris</author> << change
	<version>0.1</version>
	<type>^Localized\((.*)\)$</type>
	<editorClassName>com.hybris.cockpitng.editor.localized.LocalizedEditor</editorClassName> << change
	<handlesLocalization>true</handlesLocalization>
</editor-definition>

 

then you have to register the editor (in backoffice spring file) as the default editor, so it overrides the standard one

 

	<alias name="coreBackofficeEditorRegistry" alias="editorRegistry"/>
	<bean id="coreBackofficeEditorRegistry" parent="simpleEditorRegistry">
		<property name="defaultEditorMapping">
			<map merge="true" key-type="java.lang.String" value-type="java.lang.String">
				<entry key="^Localized\((.*)\)$" value=" TOUR CLASSNAME / EDITOR ID"/>
			</map>
		</property>
	</bean>

entry.title: Re: Optimized Error Management and Retry Strategy with Kafka on SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Thank you @ShreyasShenoy07, for this much-needed perspective. When it comes to the temporary persistence of messages for retry and reprocessing requirements, there is a significant gap between what the cloud mediation tools are offering and what is actually needed (just my opinion, open for debate). The available solutions come with many ifs and buts, so it would be better to leverage the capabilities of the available brokers to develop a design pattern to solve this, which is well explained in detail in your blog.

Your other blog utilizing MQ broker capabilities on the similar subject, was also very helpful.
Optimized Error Management and Retry Strategy with... - SAP Community

Looking forward for more contents from you 🙂

have you looked into this option?

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Error activating Business Content Sales Analysis in S...

entry.title: Where can I learn how to apply SAP IBP to optimize supply chain functions?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering SAP IBP for Demand learning journey.

Overview
SAP's Integrated Business Planning for Demand offers cutting-edge solutions that meets today's demand planning challenges. 
With our comprehensive course, you will learn how to apply SAP IBP to optimize supply chain functions and improve demand management processes. 
Discover how to generate statistical forecasts and adjust them based on intelligence, optimize daily forecasts through Demand Sensing and use key figures in planning. 
You will be introduced to the planning process of forecasting and related key figures, discovering the various forecasting algorithms, and much more.

Learning objectives
Upon completion of this training, consultants will be able to effectively utilize SAP Integrated Business Planning for Demand to generate accurate forecasts, optimize supply chain functions, manage demand planning mechanisms, and effectively respond to changes in demand using the tool's predictive and demand sensing capabilities. 

Prerequisites

  • Exploring the Core Functions in SAP IBP

  • Exploring the Tools and Features in SAP IBP
Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP S4HC Professional Services - 2nd Approval Level for Timesheet Entries?
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a business requirement to have a 2nd approval level for timesheet entries, for both internal and customer project.

The first approval level works for both project types, but we are unable to find a way to add a second approval level.

The requirement is:

Customer Projects: 

1st approval level - Project Manager (OK)

2nd approval level - Finance team (NOK)

Internal Projects: 

1st approval level - Responsible Cost Center, using BADI HCM_CATS_V1_APPROVER_DETRMN (OK)

2nd approval level - Finance team (NOK)

 

The note 3495575 - Timesheet Approval Workflow helped us for the first approval level but not for more than one.

Please let us know if a solution is available.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content:
Please read again the documentation. Isn't it clear how to solve?

Messages of type E and A are written to the log. A check is then performed to see whether the message is handled using error_message. If this is not the case, the message is also written to the log with message number 564 of the message class 00 and current background processing is terminated, whereby a message of type A executes a database rollback and a message of type E does not. Background processing is resumed when the message is handled.

It means that the "current background processing is terminated" only if you don't handle using error_message, which means "it is not terminated" if you handle using error_message. "error_message" is a hyperlink to another document.

Kiran K

Retrieval of OAuthToken failed due to: Unable to fetch refresh token from the specified token service URL. Response was: Bad credentials

I am able to replicate the same issue with postman:

The credentials are actually correct, I am able to get bearer token from my other OAuth2SAMLBearer destination for another system.

entry.title: Automatic VAS creation for WMR document category - replenishment
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Is it possible to create the VAS order automatically but for internal movement (planned replenishment)?

I've read the SAP documentation and it seems that VAS auto-creation works only for PDO or PDI documents.

I know there is a posisibility to create the VAS manually and then create the warehouse request to make the VAS activities (like described here: https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_ewm900/9.0/en-US/4c/b48fea25d1664ce10000000a15822b/frameset.htm). But the requirement is to create the WMR document at the first and then VAS (like in inbound or outbound). 

I have tried to maintain an entry in customizing: Cross-Process Settings->Value-Added Services (VAS)->Define Relevance for VAS, but there is no option to insert the WMR document category (only PDI, or PDO is possible). Even when I leave the document category empty the system doesn't create VAS order for replenishment warehouse request.

This IMG activity help says: 

    You must specify the warehouse number and document category of the warehouse request to determine the automatic VAS order ...... all other fields are optional.

The missing category seems to be the reason why VAS order in not created automatically.

I found BADI (/SCWM/EX_VAS_CREATE_FROM_DLV) for creation of VAS order from warehosue request automatically but it's not even called for WMR documents.

Have any of you ever encountered such a requirement?

Thank you in advance,

Best Regards,
Marek

entry.title: SAP Table / Structure for Production Order
entry.content:

We are building custom logic to pick Production Order Operation details.

There is no Standard Table or Structure storing values of Operation Details from Qties/Activities Tab

Most table or report store information for operation from Standard Values Tab

The above standard values for 1 Base Qty and above one is for operation qty

Regards¨Nitin

How do you handle this in your company? When is it "decoupled enough" to put it on SAP BTP? I have quite a lot of discussions about this with the S/4 SW-Architects... (i consider myself as a BTP evangelist 😉

entry.title: Handling BOM Component Precision Issues in SAP ERP and SAP Digital Manufacturing Integration
Re: Using Fiori 8D problem solving for defect created in...
entry.content:
Hi Claudio,

In S/4 HANA you can create Quality Notifications (transaction QM01) and Defects (app F2929).

You can transfer a defect to a Quality Notification item with app F2649 (Manage defects) by selecting the defect and clicking the 'Transfer defects to Notification' button.

You can transfer a defect to a problem solving document with app F2929 (Process defects) by selecting the defect and clicking the 'Start problem solving process' button.

 

As far as I know it is not possible to start the problem solving document out of a Quality Notification item.

See attached picture which shows your options. With this functionality the defect is you starting point (issue) which can be processed (small issues) and for more complex issues you could start two different process (QN or 8D). 

 

greetings, Herman

entry.title: Can customers have access to develop Business ByDesign custom solutions?

entry.title: Experience Report: ABAP Code Retreat 2024 – A Beginner's Perspective
entry.content:
ABAP Code Retreat 2024 at Inwerken AG

On September 10th, 2024, the ABAP Code Retreat took place once again in Isernhagen (Hannover). Originally launched in 2016 by @majcon and @ceedee666 this event gathers ABAP developers to code and learn together. The focus of the retreat was on Test Driven Development (TDD), with challenges like "Game of Life" or the "Bowling Kata" used to demonstrate this methodology. The event at Inwerken AG was organized and coordinated by @EnnoWulff , who ensured everything ran smoothly. As beginners, we would like to share our impressions and experiences from this inspiring workshop.

What were our expectations?

Before the event, we had mixed feelings about the ABAP Code Retreat. On one hand, we were excited to expand our knowledge, on the other, we were concerned about being overwhelmed as newcomers. We were curious to see how developers from other companies tackle problems and expected to be challenged, both by the topic and by engaging with more experienced developers.

How did it turn out?

Our initial concerns quickly disappeared once we received a thorough introduction to Test Driven Development. This helped us understand the goal of the workshop and the importance of the methodology. Thanks to the support from both the other participants and the moderator, we never felt left behind and were able to follow along at a comfortable pace.

Pair Programming was one of the biggest challenges, but also one of the most rewarding experiences. Working as a team was essential, and it helped us organize our thought process around solving problems. The alternating roles were particularly beneficial. While one person coded, the other could think through the next steps. This shift in perspective allowed both partners to contribute in different, valuable ways.

It was also fascinating to see how developers from other companies approached and solved similar challenges. And, of course, it was a great opportunity to make new connections that might extend beyond the workshop itself.

What did we learn?

A key takeaway from the retreat was an introduction to Test Driven Development (TDD). This approach is very different from typical development workflows. Instead of jumping straight into coding, TDD starts with the question: "What should my method produce as the final result?". The first step is writing a unit test that checks the desired output. Only after that do you implement the method. Finally, the unit test is used to verify whether the method's logic is correct or needs refining.

This reverse way of thinking made it much easier to code in a focused manner and spot potential problems early on. It was an "aha" moment to see how continuous testing during development.

Who should attend this workshop?

We recommend this workshop to developers looking to explore new approaches and improve their workflow. It is especially useful for ABAP developers with little to no experience in Test Driven Development (TDD), offering a hands-on opportunity to practice new methodologies that can be easily applied to everyday work.

Additionally, developers seeking to broaden their network and exchange ideas with colleagues from other companies will find this event beneficial. The collaboration during Pair Programming and the sharing of ideas provides a chance to view problems from multiple perspectives.

The ABAP Code Retreat was a valuable experience for us, helping us grow both professionally and personally. We’re looking forward to future events where we can further deepen our knowledge and make new connections.

~Ben

~@l_e_n_a 

~@Hoolya

entry.title: Re: Dump Created while fetching data TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED
entry.content:
Hello @AadivaramAbbulu 

You can split your processing into chunks using OPEN CURSOR with PACKAGE SIZE n addition and FETCH statements. Use joint select from CDHDR and CDPOS to avoid FOR ALL ENTRIES, that might also cause issues when resulting OR condition is too large.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to connect as root to a SAP CAL instance in Googl...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Please note that performing operations on VM level by using directly the hyperscaler console could lead to inconsistency of your appliance, created via SAP Cloud appliance Library UI. So that such approach is not recommended and supported by us.

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: Unlock the Full Potential of SAP Spend Connect 2024 with Pre-Conference Trainings
entry.content:
Ready to make the most of your SAP Spend Connect experience in Las Vegas? Don’t miss out on the exclusive pre-conference training sessions on October 14, 2024, designed to give you a head start! From mastering procurement strategies to optimizing expense management, these intensive workshops are your gateway to honing the skills and knowledge needed to transform your business spend management.

  • PCW2894 | SAP Ariba Category Management
  • PCW2895 | Mastering Contingent Administrative Functions in SAP Fieldglass Solutions with a Deep Dive into Rates 2.0
  • PCW2896 | Discovering SAP Integration Suite, Managed Gateway for Spend Management and SAP Business Network
  • PCW2897 | Functionalities in SAP Ariba Procurement: A New Way of Thinking

 

Join industry experts and fellow professionals in hands-on sessions that dive deep into the latest SAP tools and techniques. Elevate your expertise, stay ahead of trends, and set yourself up for success. Secure your spot now and ensure you get the maximum value from SAP Spend Connect!

Click here to explore the full agenda and register today!

entry.title: Re: How to Access SAP Build Process trigger Forms with ou...
entry.content: @Sankara1 @my understanding of the question was whether you can have anonymous forms … I was told no, but if you can I’d like to know how

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard and Universal Model Integration with SAP BW via OData Service
entry.content:
Our application can be integrated to On-premise systems such as SAP BW via Smart Data Integration (SDI) through HANA Adapter. You can see the series of steps on how it can be done here on this blog post SAP PaPM Cloud Integration with SAP BW.

Both SAP PaPM Cloud SM and UM supports OdataV4. In addition, SAP PaPM Cloud UM is designed also to support ODataV2. Therefore, on this blog post, I will show you how both SAP PaPM Cloud SM and UM can be integrated with On-premise (BW) and consume data records using OData Service with Cloud Connector.

For you to have preview how the Integration Architecture looks like, here’s a simple visualization:

ODataV4 and ODataV2 has a different step-by-step procedure on how it is being created on the BW System. Let’s start first on how we can create the service in BW for ODataV4.

Creation of an OdataV4 service in SEGW:

With NW750, in SEGW, ODataV4 project can be created. Though it is not recommended as described on SAP Note SAP Note 2485370, project creation will still go through, and users can generate runtime objects that can be use on the service that will be created.

Once the Project is created. Users can import the DDIC structure to the Data Model. In this example, I will be using the DDIC Table generated from an existing ADSO

  1. On the created Project in SEGW, press right click to Data Model
  2. Choose Import
  3. Then choose DDIC Structure
  4. Add Entity name
  5. Search the existing Table generated from ADSO
  6. Choose Inbound Table generated from ADSO
  7. Next

Once the DDIC table was chosen, users may now proceed on fields mapping that will be required for the scenario.

  1. Choose the required fields
  2. Next
  3. Ensure that you have Key field
  4. Finish

On this Import example above, I have imported an existing ADSO using BW Fields rather than BW InfoObject. as the system does not allow fields that contains “/BIC” as its technical field name for ODataV4 project type in SEGW.

For more information, please refer to the official documentation Importing a DDIC Structure.

Once everything is mapped, users can generate the runtime objects which are Service ID, Classes that will be needed for the service creation.

With the created OdataV4 Project. We can now move to Registration and Publishing of the Service.

A.      Registration of the ODataV4 Service

1. Go to SAP back-end Service Administration via tcode /iwbep/v4_admin. Then Register Group

2. Fill up the necessary details to create the Service Group.

3. After filling up the creation of Service group, the system will ask if you would like to register an existing service. Fill up the information with the Generated Runtime objects details from previous steps.

Note: We have used the Extension of Model and Provider as changes are still needed for the generated runtime objects. Since PaPM Cloud SM Model View Odata only supports OdataV4, users have to perform a Code based implementation. 

SAMPLE CODE:

METHOD /iwbep/if_v4_dp_basic~read_entity_list.
    DATA: lv_entity_type_name TYPE /iwbep/if_v4_med_element=>ty_e_med_internal_name,
          ls_done_list        TYPE /iwbep/if_v4_requ_basic_list=>ty_s_todo_process_list,
          ls_todo_list        TYPE /iwbep/if_v4_requ_basic_list=>ty_s_todo_list,
          lt_data             TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF /BIC/AZQAM_DEMO2.
    io_request->get_entity_type( IMPORTING ev_entity_type_name = lv_entity_type_name ).
    io_request->get_todos( IMPORTING es_todo_list = ls_todo_list ).

    CASE lv_entity_type_name.
      WHEN 'DEMO_ENTITY'.
        SELECT * FROM /BIC/AZQAM_DEMO2
         INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @lt_data.

        IF ls_todo_list-return-count = abap_true.
          io_response->set_count( lines( lt_data ) ).
        ENDIF.

        io_response->set_busi_data( it_busi_data = lt_data ).
        io_response->set_is_done( ls_done_list ).
      WHEN OTHERS.
        super->/iwbep/if_v4_dp_basic~read_entity_list( io_request  = io_request
                                                       io_response = io_response ).
    ENDCASE.
  ENDMETHOD.

Disclaimer: For any issues with the Odata V4 code based implementation, please refer to blog post: OData V4 code based implementation - Overview.

4. Once done, we can proceed on assigning the service to the created Group.

B.  Publish the ODataV4 Service

1. Go to SAP Gateway Service Administration via tcode /iwfnd/v4_admin. Then choose Publish Service Groups.

2. Search and select the Service Group created from previous steps. Then choose, Publish Service Groups

3. Once the service is published, go to transaction code /iwbep/v4_admin. Choose the assigned service created from previous steps and do Service Test to Gateway Client like the image below.

4. Choose the Entity set from the project created from SEGW. With this, it will generate the request URL .

 5. Execute the request URL to show the data records available in OdataV4 format.

With these results, we can now proceed on the next steps to create a connection between our BW and SAP PaPM Cloud SM / UM. But before that, let me show you first how we can create the ODataV2 service in BW.

Creation of an OdataV2 service in SEGW:

With the current version of NW750, in SEGW transaction code, OdataV2 service can be generated.

For the step-by-step procedure, you may refer to Creating a Service Builder Project.

Once the Project is created. Users can import the DDIC structure to the Data Model. It could be your ADSO, Table or Views.

For more information, please refer to the official documentation Importing a DDIC Structure.

If mapping of all fields and setting a primary are done, users may now proceed on mapping the Service Implementation to the Data Source.

  1. Under the project created, expand the folder Service Implementation.
  2. Right Click to the Entity Set generated from Import.
  3. Choose Map data to source.

After the previous Step C, users need to maintain the Data Source Attributes.

  1. Choose the Type ‘Business Entity’
  2. Go to Help button
  3. Choose SADL Model Type ‘DDIC Dictionary’ (This one is used for our example)
  4. Choose the Model that was imported from previous step
  5. Press Continue
  6. Press again Continue to finalize the mapping to data source

Upon pressing the continue button from Step F. The UI will look like the image below. To finalize the mapping, users need to click the Generate Mapping button on top of the table of the mapped fields.

Note: Unlike in ODataV4 project type. ODataV2 in SEGW accepts BW ADSO that contains InfoObject fields with “/BIC” as its mapped technical name like the image below.

Once all the fields are mapped, users now need to Generate the Runtime Objects.

  1. Press Generate Runtime Objects.
  2. All values on the pop-up windows are system generated, users may proceed to press Continue button.
  3. Add $TMP on package.
  4. Press Save button

When it is saved, it will provide a message log below that the Runtime Objects for the Project created were generated successfully.

A.      Publishing of the ODataV2 Service

The Runtime Objects and Service were successfully generated. But this still needs to be published for the user to make it appear on the Active services in our BW system.

  1. Go to transaction code: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.
  2. Choose Add Service button.

      3. Search for the Technical Service name using the Service generated from pressing Generate Runtime Objects from previous step.

  1. Select the searched Service.
  2. Choose Add Selected Service
  3. Add $TMP
  4. Press Continue

B.      Calling the ODataV2 Service

Since the service is already created, registered, and published, users may now proceed to call the ODataV2 service.

  1. Go back to transaction code: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and filter outby searching the service created.
  2. Calling the service in the Gateway Client
  1. Double click the published service.
  2. Under ICF Nodes section, choose Gateway Client 

     3. In the Gateway Client, press Execute button to verify if the service is successfully implemented. It will return status_code 200 means that the service can be called successfully.
     4. Let’s call now the Entity set that was imported from previous step.

  1. On the header, press EntitySets.
  2. Choose the EntitySet created from previous steps.
  3. Press Execute button.
  4. See all available data records in the HTTP Response in XML format.

With the successful execution, this service path can now be called in our SAP PaPM Cloud UM as this is the model that supports ODataV2.

Let’s proceed now on the creation of Connection between your BW and SAP PaPM Cloud SM or UM.

Creation of Connection in Cloud Connector

A.      Registering your SAP PaPM Cloud SM Instance or Tenant

On registration of your tenant in the Cloud Connector, please refer to the blog post Integration: Setup SAP PaPM Cloud to use RFA FI-GL and FI-GLI to post to your ECC under the section of A. in Cloud Connector: Registering your SAP PaPM Cloud Instance or Tenant.

B. In Cloud Connector: Register your internal System in the created Sub-account

  1. Choose the Subaccount that needs to be connected to the On-premise system
  2. Choose Cloud to On-Premise from the left menu pane
  3. Follow the steps that are being shown in the GIF below:

C.      In Cloud Connector: Register the resources of the added Internal System

The resources to be registered is the URL Path /sap. This is for the connection to know where the request will direct.

SAP BTP: Double check the Cloud Connector

If Sections A to C were completed, you should see the same reflecting in BTP > Cloud Connectors as Connected / Available

Connected
this means your Subaccount is connected in Cloud Connector

Available
this means your Internal system has resources added path (like /sap)

SAP BTP: Create Destination

Now that everything is configured in Cloud connector, Destination Administrator may now proceed to create a new Destination (HTTP Destination)

  1. Under your subaccount, go to Destinations.
  2. Choose New Destination
  3. Fill the Destination Fields with information from your HTTP Connection
  4. Add the Client of the Internal system in the Properties
  5. Save the configuration.

SAP PaPM Cloud SM Modeling – OdataV4

1. Create the Connection according to your Destination and Service details.

2. In your Modeling screen, create now the Model View OData maintaining the connection created from previous step. 

 3. By choosing the connection, PaPM Cloud SM will be able to detect all available fields from the Odata service that Modeler can use in the Fields Mapping.

With the successful connection, activation and execution will return a successful log. Modeler can now validate the consumed data records in the Show screen and use it for Complex Calculations and Reporting in SAP PaPM Cloud SM.

As mentioned from the introduction of this blog post, ODataV2 is not supported in SAP PaPM Cloud SM as it only supports ODataV4 service. See documentation: Model View OData .

With this, let’s move to the next procedures of this blog post.

SAP PaPM Cloud UM Modeling - ODataV2

  1. Creation of Connection that will use the Destination created from previous step.
  1. Add the Connection ID.
  2. Put the description of the Connection.
  3. Use the destination created from previous step.
  4. Add the existing service path from generated service.
  5. Add Default Key Field.
  6. Choose Create

With the successful creation, the EntitySet created from previous step will be visible in the UI of SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model where the user can perform the field mapping.

For further information with the creation of Connection and mapping, please refer to Manage Connections: Odata Service.

2. Fetch the data records of the OdataV2 service using Reader function.

In SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model, to fetch the available data records in an Odata Service, users need to create a Reader function on top of the synced Model Entity from our connection.

Users may now execute the Reader function. Data records are available under the Show Data screen of the Reader and the Synced Model Entity from Connection. See steps from the image below:

Reader:

 Synced Model Entity:

Now, I have shown you how ODataV4 in SAP PaPM Cloud SM and ODataV2 in SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model are successfully consumed via connection. Since Universal Model also supports ODataV4. Let me show it to you as well.

Technically, the steps are the same, users just must change the service path that is being used for ODataV4. Let’s proceed on the next step below

SAP PaPM Cloud UM Modeling – OdataV4

The service root used from the creation of Connection in SAP PaPM Cloud SM can be used as well in the creation of connection in SAP PaPM Cloud UM since we are going to consume the same ODataV4 service.

  1. Creation of Connection that will use the Destination created from previous step.
  1. Add the Connection ID.
  2. Put the description of the Connection.
  3. Use the destination created from previous step.
  4. Add the existing service path from generated service. (Same from PaPM Cloud SM)
  5. Add Default Key Field.
  6. Choose Create

Once the connection is created, it will show the available Entity set we have from the BW system then users may now perform the mapping of relevant fields for the scenario.

For further information with the creation of Connection and mapping, please refer to Manage Connections: Odata Service.

2. Fetch the data records of the OdataV4 service using Reader function.

Similar from ODataV2, in OdataV4, Reader Function should be RUN as well to fetch the data records to our Synced Model Entity and to the Reader function. Follow the steps in the image below for reference.

Reader:

 Model Entity:

Thank you so much for reaching here until the last step of our integration topic! After reading all through the steps of this blog post, we can conclude that in Integration between BW and SAP PaPM Cloud via OData service is possible with scenarios listed below:

  • SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model – it only supports ODataV4 service.
  • SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model – it supports ODataV2 and ODataV4 services.

I hope now you have learned the other way on how to integrate BW to our SAP PaPM Cloud Application.

See you again soon! On to the next integration topic 😊.

entry.title: Re: How to add oAttachments2 to oPurchaseQuotations using...
entry.content:
Hi Ankit:

Thanks for your response.

So, I need to execute first the oAttachment2 xml file and this will create the integer value for these attachments, and then I look for this value in a search query to use it in oPurchaseQuotation? Or what is the process to do this?

entry.title: Re: Migration of EHANA to Datasphere by leveraging Native HANA Cloud Instance of Datasphere Tenant
entry.content: Thank you @Jinal_Patel for the blog on migrating EHANA to Datasphere using a native HANA Cloud instance. Adding to @Vivek-RR's suggestions, while the migration strategy outlined presents a compelling approach, it's essential to consider certain restrictions associated with the HANA Cloud tenant of Datasphere. Let me add some comments from the Multi-Model & ML aspect of SAP HANA Cloud. According to SAP Notes 3463689, 3349720, and 2994416, it is important to highlight that directly leveraging advanced analytics capabilities like the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), Automated Predictive Library (APL), JSON Document Store or the Vector Engine (multi-model engines, also involves Graph Engine) can be challenging within this environment. These features are crucial for complex machine learning and AI-driven use cases, such as predictive modeling and vector-based similarity searches. However, due to certain limitations in Datasphere’s HANA Cloud tenant - like restricted access to administrative tools, custom configuration options, or the inability to fully utilize all the native functionalities of a stand alone HANA Cloud database - implementing these advanced analytics capabilities becomes less straightforward. These points are crucial for anyone planning a migration to be fully aware of and to assess how these restrictions might affect their specific use cases and system requirements. For further clarification on an effective migration strategy, I will defer it to the migration experts on my HANA Cloud team - samarjit.saha@sap.com

entry.title: Re: How to create, open and share Bookmarks in SAP Analytics Cloud?
entry.content:
Thanks @neenulalu94 , @MKreitlein .

I observed another issue. I have 10 pages in my story but in view mode 7th  page should be the first users can see. I have created a bookmark by opening the 7th page and made it default view for global bookmark but when I open in view mode it by default shows the first page only. Is there any way we can customize bookmark to show a particular page out of all existing pages.

entry.title: Missing "yellow total line" and "Change layout button" in ME51N/ME52N
entry.content:
Hi all,

could somebody help me? It's urgent.

User cannot see the yellow total line in ME51N/ME52N (Display/Change Purchase Requisition) and also not the "Change Layout Button". The yellow total line is more important. To add it, he wanted to change the layout but he hasn't any option to do it. He's using the normal GUI (ERP system). What could be the issue?

Thank you and Regards

Zoran

entry.title: FD08 change log for approver user
entry.content: Is there a way to check which user confirmed changes to customer master record via transaction FD08? In the change history I can only see a user that has no access to FD08 so there must have been someone else who confirmed changes.

entry.title: Re: SAP Tech Bytes: Use Destinations During Local Development - Cloud Foundry Basics #4
entry.content:
Hi, all

I know it's over a year, but is there still no way to connect a non-BAS environment to the subaccount level destinations? I'm struggling a bit with this.
I'm thinking maybe there's some way to create a service level destination that would point to the subaccount destination? I haven't been able to figure it out, though.

Best regards

entry.title: SAP Encryption Key - Database - SSFS
entry.content:
Hi all,

In the command line:

rsecssfx generatekey -getPlainValueToConsole

What is the format of the key generated? (AES 256 bits)?

Can this command

rsecssfx changekey <new key>

be used with a key generated externally? with openssl or other? In this case what would be the format.

Kind regards,
ily83

entry.title: Re: Dump Created while fetching data TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED
entry.content:
Also SELECT ... PACKAGE SIZE ... is possible.

ABAP documentation: https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abapinto_clause.htm#!ABAP_ONE_ADD@1@
